# Music Habits

This is my current listening data from apple music for the band *Ghost*.

# Data Analysis

Import any necessary libraries.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)
library(moderndive)
```
:::


As usual, I import the data from my apple music account. 


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ghost <- read_tsv("Ghost.txt", show_col_types = FALSE)

# cleanup data
ghost <- ghost |> 
    janitor::clean_names() |> 
    janitor::remove_empty() |> 
    filter(!is.na(plays), plays > 0) |> 
    filter(!is.na(duration), !is.na(plays)) |> 
    rename(duration = time)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
value for "which" not specified, defaulting to c("rows", "cols")
```


:::

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
Warning: There was 1 warning in `filter()`.
ℹ In argument: `!is.na(duration)`.
Caused by warning in `is.na()`:
! is.na() applied to non-(list or vector) of type 'closure'
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
ghost |> 
  head()
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 6 × 22

```


:::
:::


Plot the data in a histogram.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(ghost, aes(x=plays, fill=album)) +
    geom_histogram(binwidth=5, na.rm = TRUE)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-3-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


::: callout-note
The data is **uni-model** and **right-skewed**, therefore, the **median** will be used here.
:::

Next, I create a table of some useful values.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ghost <- ghost |> 
  mutate(total_seconds = duration * plays)
ghost |>
  summarise(
    median_plays = median(plays),
    mean_plays = mean(plays),
    total_plays = sum(plays),
    total_hours = sum(total_seconds, na.rm = TRUE) / 3600
  ) -> ghost_summary

ghost_summary |> 
  kable()
```

::: {.cell-output-display}


| median_plays| mean_plays| total_plays| total_hours|
|------------:|----------:|-----------:|-----------:|
|            5|   27.91525|        1647|    117.8828|


:::
:::


These values tell us that:

    - 50% of songs are played less than 5 times.
    - across the selection of music, if the plays were evenly distributed, then the average number of plays would be $~28$ times.
    - the combined number of plays across all songs is $1647$.
    - I spent about $118$ hours listening.
